summary: Gruber promotes his article for O'Reilly's Mac DevCenter about scripting rectangular selections in BBEdit 7. The piece highlights how BBEdit 7's AppleScript support allows creating 'rectangular' selections that aren't actually rectangular. He uses a screenshot to demonstrate this quirky capability, finding amusement in the naming contradiction.
key_points: Gruber wrote a technical article for O'Reilly's Mac DevCenter on BBEdit 7's rectangular selections via AppleScript
BBEdit 7's AppleScript interface allows creating 'rectangular' selections that are not actually rectangular in shape
The post serves as a brief promotional pointer to the full article published elsewhere
money_quotes: I put quotes around rectangular because, as the following screenshot indicates, you're not limited to actual rectangles when you create rectangular selections via AppleScript.
key_insight: BBEdit 7's AppleScript support for rectangular selections is more flexible than the name implies, allowing non-rectangular selection shapes.
